classification_rules.rule: Tag documents at leaf level only. Parent branches are computed from leaf tags at build time and are never assigned directly.

A document may carry multiple leaf tags when it genuinely regulates more than one domain. A virtual asset framework that also sets rules for the underlying distributed ledger technology tags digital_finance.virtual_assets and ai_emerging_tech.blockchain_web3.
No general, other, or miscellaneous categories exist anywhere in the tree. If a document fits no leaf, the tree has a gap. Propose a new leaf with a scope statement instead of forcing a bad fit.
Every node carries a scope statement defining what is in and what is out. Classify against the scope statement, never against the label alone.
Personal data breach notification and response duties sit under data_privacy.personal_data_protection, never under cybersecurity, even when the instrument is issued by a cybersecurity authority.

Offences against information systems, such as unauthorised access and computer misuse, sit under cybersecurity.cybercrime. Online content offences sit under online_content.content_standards_moderation, even when both appear in the same statute; such a statute tags both leaves.

A royal or emiri decree that merely promulgates a law is not a separate record. It belongs in the law's citation metadata. A decree is recorded separately only when it carries substantive provisions of its own.

The dataset covers digital regulation only: instruments whose primary subject is digital technology, data, networks, online activity, or digitally delivered services. Non digital instruments are out of scope even when issued by a digital authority. Instruments in traditional domains enter only when they specifically regulate the digital channel or technology.

instrument_classes.label: Law
instrument_classes.scope: Primary legislation enacted through the jurisdiction's constitutional lawmaking process and carrying the highest force below the constitution. Out of scope: implementing or executive regulations issued under a law, which belong under regulation, and decrees that merely promulgate a law, which live in the law's citation metadata.

jurisdiction_forms.AE: Federal law or federal decree law
jurisdiction_forms.BH: Law, issued by decree law or law

instrument_classes.label: Decree
instrument_classes.scope: A royal or emiri decree carrying substantive provisions of its own, such as establishing an authority or ordering a specific measure. Out of scope: decrees that merely promulgate a law, which are recorded in the law's citation metadata, and federal decree laws, which belong under law.

instrument_classes.label: Regulation
instrument_classes.scope: An implementing or executive regulation issued under an enabling law to give it operational effect. Must link to its enabling law through an implements relationship. Out of scope: standalone rules issued by a sectoral regulator without a single enabling statute, which belong under decision or rulebook_module.

instrument_classes.label: Resolution
instrument_classes.scope: A cabinet or ministerial resolution with general binding effect, including resolutions that designate competent authorities or set sectoral requirements. Out of scope: resolutions whose sole content is an executive regulation, which belong under regulation, and instruments of sectoral regulators, which belong under decision.

jurisdiction_forms.SA: Council of Ministers resolution or ministerial resolution
jurisdiction_forms.QA: Council of Ministers decision or ministerial decision
jurisdiction_forms.KW: Council of Ministers decision or ministerial resolution

instrument_classes.label: Regulator decision
instrument_classes.scope: A binding instrument issued by a sectoral regulator within its statutory mandate, such as licensing conditions, technical rules, or administrative decisions of general application. Out of scope: cabinet and ministerial instruments, which belong under resolution, and regulator guidance without binding force, which belongs under guideline.

instrument_classes.label: Circular
instrument_classes.scope: A binding instruction issued by a regulator or ministry to its supervised population or subordinate bodies, typically narrower and more operational than a decision. Out of scope: circulars that only explain existing rules without imposing duties, which belong under guideline.

instrument_classes.label: Rulebook module
instrument_classes.scope: A module of a regulator's consolidated rulebook, recorded at module level because modules are amended independently. Covers structures such as the CBB Rulebook. Out of scope: standalone regulator instruments outside a rulebook structure, which belong under decision or circular.

instrument_classes.label: National strategy
instrument_classes.scope: A government wide strategy setting direction, targets, and institutional responsibilities for a policy domain. Out of scope: sector policies of a single ministry or regulator, which belong under policy.

instrument_classes.label: Policy
instrument_classes.scope: A stated position or operating policy of a ministry or regulator that guides decisions without creating directly enforceable duties. Out of scope: government wide strategies, which belong under national_strategy, and documents that explain compliance steps, which belong under guideline.

instrument_classes.label: Guideline
instrument_classes.scope: Guidance explaining how to interpret or comply with binding instruments, or recommending practices, without itself creating enforceable duties. Out of scope: codes that operate on a comply or explain basis, which belong under code_of_practice, and binding circulars.

instrument_classes.label: Framework
instrument_classes.scope: A structured reference model organising principles, controls, or maturity levels for a domain, intended for adoption rather than enforcement. Out of scope: frameworks made mandatory by a binding instrument, which keep this class but carry conditionally_binding status with an enabled_by link.

instrument_classes.label: Handbook
instrument_classes.scope: An operational manual consolidating procedures and explanatory material for practitioners. Out of scope: regulator rulebooks with binding force, which belong under rulebook_module.

instrument_classes.label: Code of practice
instrument_classes.scope: A code of expected conduct for a sector or activity, often operating on a comply or explain basis. Out of scope: codes incorporated by reference into a binding instrument, which keep this class but carry conditionally_binding status with an enabled_by link.

instrument_classes.label: Standard
instrument_classes.scope: A technical or management standard specifying requirements or controls, voluntary unless incorporated by reference. Out of scope: none by form; standards made mandatory carry conditionally_binding status with an enabled_by link to the incorporating instrument.

instrument_classes.label: Consultation paper
instrument_classes.scope: A document published to gather public or industry input on a proposed instrument or policy. Out of scope: the resulting instrument itself, which is recorded separately and linked through resulted_from_consultation.

instrument_classes.label: White paper
instrument_classes.scope: An analytical or position paper published by a public body to inform debate or signal regulatory direction, without proposing specific draft text. Out of scope: papers seeking structured feedback on draft rules, which belong under consultation_paper.

binding_statuses.label: Binding
binding_statuses.scope: The instrument creates directly enforceable legal duties within its scope of application.

binding_statuses.label: Non binding
binding_statuses.scope: The instrument guides or informs without creating enforceable duties.

binding_statuses.label: Conditionally binding
binding_statuses.scope: The instrument binds only through another instrument, such as a standard incorporated by reference or a comply or explain code. Requires an enabled_by relationship to the instrument that triggers the force.

lifecycle.label: In consultation
lifecycle.scope: Published as a draft or consultation and not yet enacted.

lifecycle.label: Enacted
lifecycle.scope: Formally issued but not yet in force, typically awaiting an effective date or implementing measures.

lifecycle.label: In force
lifecycle.scope: Currently effective and enforceable, or operative in the case of non binding instruments.

lifecycle.label: Amended
lifecycle.scope: In force with one or more amendments recorded through amended_by relationships. The record describes the instrument as amended.

lifecycle.label: Repealed
lifecycle.scope: Formally abrogated by a later instrument, recorded through a repealed_by relationship.

lifecycle.label: Superseded
lifecycle.scope: Replaced in practice by a later instrument without formal repeal. Common for strategies and guidance that are quietly replaced. Distinct from repealed, which requires formal abrogation.

relationship_types.scope: The source instrument changes the text or effect of the target instrument.
The source instrument formally abrogates the target instrument in whole or in part.
The source instrument replaces the target in practice without formal repeal.
The source instrument gives operational effect to the target enabling instrument, such as an executive regulation implementing its law.
The source consultation paper seeks input on what became the target instrument.
The source instrument derives binding force from the target, such as a standard incorporated by reference. Required for conditionally_binding instruments.
The source instrument materially relies on the target without amending or implementing it. Use sparingly, only where the reference matters for compliance.

themes.label: Data & privacy
themes.scope: Regulation of personal data and the data economy: protection of personal data, movement of data across borders, and governance of data as a national resource. Out of scope: health sector specific data rules, which sit under digital_health.health_data, and security of systems and networks, which sits under cybersecurity.

children.label: Personal data protection
children.scope: General data protection laws and their implementing instruments: lawful bases, data subject rights, controller and processor duties, registration, and personal data breach notification and response duties, which sit here and never under cybersecurity. General data protection law applied to health data also sits here. Out of scope: transfer and localisation rules, which sit under crossborder_data_flows, and health sector specific data rules, which sit under digital_health.health_data.

children.label: Cross-border data flows
children.scope: Rules on moving data across borders: transfer mechanisms, adequacy decisions, and data localisation requirements, whether set in data protection law or in sectoral instruments. Out of scope: the general processing duties of the underlying data protection law, which sit under personal_data_protection.

children.label: Data governance & open data
children.scope: Governance of data as a national resource: national data strategies, data offices and their mandates, data sharing and classification frameworks, and open data policies. Out of scope: protection of personal data, which sits under personal_data_protection, and digital delivery of government services, which sits under digital_government.egovernment_services.

themes.label: AI & emerging technologies
themes.scope: Governance of artificial intelligence and other emerging digital technologies: AI strategies and ethics, generative AI, autonomous and smart systems, connected devices, and blockchain as a technology. Out of scope: crypto assets as a regulated financial activity, which sit under digital_finance.virtual_assets, and personal data aspects of AI systems, which also tag data_privacy.personal_data_protection.

children.label: AI governance & ethics
children.scope: National AI strategies, AI ethics principles, AI risk and governance frameworks, and regulatory sandboxes for novel non financial technologies, which fold into this scope. Out of scope: instruments specific to generative AI, which sit under generative_ai, and fintech sandboxes, which sit under digital_finance.fintech_sandboxes.

children.label: Generative AI
children.scope: Instruments specific to generative AI systems: adoption and usage guidelines, disclosure and provenance duties for generated content, and sector rules for generative models. Out of scope: general AI ethics and governance frameworks that mention generative AI in passing, which sit under ai_governance_ethics.

children.label: Autonomous & smart systems
children.scope: Regulation of the autonomy itself: autonomous vehicle and drone autonomy rules, robotics, and smart city technologies. Out of scope: transport and operator licensing that does not regulate the digital technology, which is outside the dataset, and AI ethics frameworks of general application, which sit under ai_governance_ethics.

children.label: IoT & connected devices
children.scope: Regulation of internet of things services and connected devices, including the CST and TDRA IoT frameworks: device registration, connectivity rules, and IoT service provider duties. Out of scope: spectrum allocation, which sits under telecom_digital_infrastructure.spectrum, and security controls of general application, which sit under cybersecurity.security_controls_frameworks.

children.label: Blockchain & Web3
children.scope: Blockchain as a technology: government DLT strategies, smart contract recognition, and distributed ledger infrastructure. Boundary: crypto as a financial activity sits under digital_finance.virtual_assets; an instrument doing both tags both leaves. Out of scope: payment systems on conventional rails, which sit under digital_finance.payments_emoney.

themes.label: Cybersecurity
themes.scope: Security of systems, networks, and digital infrastructure: security controls and frameworks, critical infrastructure protection, incident reporting, and offences against information systems. Out of scope: personal data breach notification duties, which sit under data_privacy.personal_data_protection even when issued by a cybersecurity authority, and online content offences, which sit under online_content.content_standards_moderation.

children.label: Security controls & frameworks
children.scope: Baseline and sectoral security controls and national cybersecurity frameworks, such as the NCA Essential Cybersecurity Controls; encryption and cryptography standards fold into this scope. Out of scope: controls specific to designated critical infrastructure, which sit under critical_infrastructure, and incident reporting duties, which sit under incident_reporting.

children.label: Critical infrastructure
children.scope: Protection of critical information infrastructure: designation regimes, sector specific resilience requirements, and obligations on operators of essential digital services. Out of scope: baseline controls of general application, which sit under security_controls_frameworks.

children.label: Incident reporting
children.scope: Duties to report system compromise and cybersecurity incidents to authorities: reporting channels, thresholds, and timelines. Covers system compromise reporting only; personal data breach notification sits under data_privacy.personal_data_protection.

children.label: Cybercrime
children.scope: Offences against information systems: unauthorised access, computer misuse, unlawful interception, and the related criminal procedure in cybercrime statutes. Out of scope: online content offences in the same statutes, which sit under online_content.content_standards_moderation.

themes.label: Telecommunications & digital infrastructure
themes.scope: Regulation of communications networks and the infrastructure layer of the digital economy: licensing and markets, spectrum, OTT services, cloud and data centres, and internet governance. Out of scope: content carried over networks, which sits under online_content, and security duties of network operators, which sit under cybersecurity.

children.label: Licensing & markets
children.scope: Licensing of telecom networks and services, sector competition and market rules, interconnection and access, and consumer rules issued by telecom regulators. Out of scope: spectrum assignment, which sits under spectrum, and rules specific to OTT services, which sit under ott_digital_comms.

children.label: Spectrum
children.scope: Allocation, assignment, and pricing of radio spectrum, national spectrum strategies, and technical conditions for radio equipment and frequency use. Out of scope: general network and service licensing, which sits under licensing_markets.

children.label: OTT & digital communication services
children.label_ar: خدمات الاتصال الرقمي والخدمات عبر الإنترنت
children.scope: Rules for over the top and digital communication services, including VoIP permissions and restrictions and messaging service rules, a distinctly GCC regulatory topic. Out of scope: licensing of the underlying networks, which sits under licensing_markets, and standards for communicated content, which sit under online_content.

children.label: Cloud & data centres
children.scope: Cloud computing regulatory frameworks such as the CST cloud framework and Bahrain's cloud rules, cloud service provider registration, and data centre licensing and classification. Out of scope: government cloud first policy, which sits under digital_government.egovernment_services, and data localisation, which sits under data_privacy.crossborder_data_flows.

children.label: Internet governance & domain names
children.scope: Country code top level domain regulation, domain name registration rules, and national internet governance arrangements. Out of scope: website content and online media licensing duties, which sit under online_content.

themes.label: Digital government & identity
themes.scope: Digital transformation of the state and the identity layer it depends on: e government services and programmes, national digital identity, and trust services. Out of scope: governance of data as a national resource, which sits under data_privacy.data_governance_open_data, and security controls for government systems, which sit under cybersecurity.security_controls_frameworks.

children.label: E-government services
children.scope: Digital delivery of government services: e services regulation, digital transformation programmes and enablement policies, and government cloud first policy. Out of scope: national digital ID, e signatures, and trust services, which sit under digital_identity_trust.

children.label: Digital identity & trust services
children.scope: National digital identity schemes, electronic signatures, electronic transactions and trust services laws, and trust service provider rules. Out of scope: electronic contracting rules aimed at online selling, which sit under digital_economy.ecommerce.

themes.label: Digital economy & platforms
themes.scope: Regulation of commercial activity conducted through digital channels: e commerce, online platforms and intermediaries, and digital tax administration. Out of scope: payment services, which sit under digital_finance.payments_emoney, and online advertising rules, which sit under online_content.online_advertising_influencers.

children.label: E-commerce
children.scope: E commerce laws and licensing: online selling requirements, electronic contracting in trade, digital consumer disclosures, and marketplace seller duties. Out of scope: platform governance duties beyond selling, which sit under online_platforms, and payment services, which sit under digital_finance.payments_emoney.

children.label: Online platforms
children.scope: Duties of online platforms and intermediaries: app store rules, sharing economy platform regulation, and intermediary registration and liability regimes. Out of scope: content moderation duties, which sit under online_content.content_standards_moderation, and online selling rules, which sit under ecommerce.

children.label: E-invoicing & digital tax administration
children.scope: Mandates digitising tax administration, such as ZATCA's Fatoora e invoicing regime and its GCC equivalents: technical specifications, integration phases, and compliance timelines. Out of scope: the underlying tax base and rates, which are non digital and outside the dataset.

themes.label: Digital finance
themes.scope: Financial activity conducted through digital channels and digital assets: payments and e money, open banking, virtual assets, and fintech sandboxes. Out of scope: blockchain as a technology, which sits under ai_emerging_tech.blockchain_web3; an instrument regulating both the technology and the financial activity tags both.

children.label: Payments & e-money
children.scope: Licensing and conduct rules for payment services, e money issuance, stored value facilities, and digital wallets. Out of scope: open banking data sharing frameworks, which sit under open_banking, and banking regulation without a digital channel focus, which is outside the dataset.

children.label: Open banking
children.scope: Open banking and open finance frameworks: API standards, data sharing between banks and third party providers, and licensing of account information and payment initiation services. Out of scope: general payment service licensing, which sits under payments_emoney.

children.label: Virtual assets
children.scope: Crypto and virtual assets as regulated financial activity: token classification, exchange and custody licensing, VASP regimes, VARA rulebooks, and the AML duties of virtual asset service providers, which tag here. Out of scope: blockchain as a technology, which sits under ai_emerging_tech.blockchain_web3; an instrument doing both tags both.

children.label: Fintech sandboxes
children.scope: Regulatory sandboxes and innovation testing frameworks operated by financial regulators: eligibility, testing conditions, and exit paths. Out of scope: sandboxes for non financial technologies, which sit under ai_emerging_tech.ai_governance_ethics.

themes.label: Online content & media
themes.scope: Regulation of content and media activity online: licensing of online media, content standards and moderation, online advertising, and online gaming. Out of scope: broadcast and print regulation without a digital channel focus, which is outside the dataset, and the networks carrying the content, which sit under telecom_digital_infrastructure.

children.label: Online media licensing
children.scope: Licensing and registration of online media activity, such as the UAE electronic media regulation and Saudi online journalism licensing. Out of scope: rules on what content is permissible, which sit under content_standards_moderation.

children.label: Content standards & moderation
children.scope: Rules on permissible online content, content classification, moderation and takedown duties, and online content offences, which sit here and never under cybersecurity.cybercrime. Out of scope: licensing of the media outlet itself, which sits under online_media_licensing.

children.label: Online advertising & influencers
children.scope: Online advertising standards, influencer licensing and disclosure duties, and platform advertising rules. Out of scope: general content standards, which sit under content_standards_moderation, and digital consumer disclosures in online selling, which sit under digital_economy.ecommerce.

children.label: Gaming & esports
children.scope: Regulation of online gaming and esports, including licensed online gaming regimes such as the UAE GCGRA, game content classification, and esports organisation. Out of scope: gambling offences in general penal law without a digital gaming regime, which are outside the dataset.

themes.label: Digital health
themes.scope: Digitally delivered health services and health sector data: telehealth and digital health platforms, and health sector specific data rules. Out of scope: general data protection law applied to health data, which sits under data_privacy.personal_data_protection, and non digital health regulation, which is outside the dataset.

children.label: Telehealth & digital health services
children.label_ar: الرعاية الصحية عن بعد والخدمات الصحية الرقمية
children.scope: Telehealth and telemedicine rules, licensing of digital health platforms and services, and standards for remote care delivery. Out of scope: licensing of physical facilities and practitioners without a digital channel focus, which is outside the dataset.

children.label: Health data
children.scope: Health sector specific data rules: electronic health record systems, health information exchange, and health data classification issued by health authorities. General data protection law applied to health data stays under data_privacy.personal_data_protection.
